The disease was first discovered in 1888 by a man who died in Kinshasa in what was then the Belgian Congo. The organism was finally linked to the simian immunodeficiency virus (SIV) found in chimpanzees in central Africa. SIV probably entered the human population as a result of human hunting and hunting among others. It came to global attention after doctors started receiving reports of rare cases of pneumonia, cancer and other illnesses in young gay homosexual men and injection drug users. TargetRLF1: Genital Herpes or Herpes Simplex Virus (HSV), also known as herpes is a highly contagious disease. It is transmitted from an infected person to the other through direct contact. Herpes simplex-1 (HSV-1) is predominantly oral, therefore it can be transmitted even if it occurs outside the mouth (oral herpes or cold sores). It can also cause genital herpes. Most people have no symptoms or only mild symptoms. However, it is still possible for herpes to cause painful blisters or ulcers (lesions). Pregnant women with HSV-1 are not at risk of acquiring HSV-2. HSV-2 is mainly transmitted during sex through contact with genital or anal areas not covered by the condom. In rare circumstances, herpes (HSV-1 and HSV-2) canbe transmitted via kissing, skin-to-skin contact can cause problems with brain and spinal cord function, including skin rashes, fever, and heart and brain.

TargetRLF1: Hepatitis B is an infection that originated in animals and passed into human hosts. It is closely related to the simian immunodeficiency virus (HBV) found in chimpanzees in central Africa. To date, the earliest known case of HIV-1 infection in human blood is from a sample taken in 1959 from physician Alfred Donne, who discovered it in Kinshasa in what was then the Belgian Congo. The origin of the Human Immunodeficiency Virus (HIV) has been a subject of scientific research and debate since the virus was identified in the 1980s. It came to global attention after doctors started receiving reports of rare cases of pneumonia, cancer and other illnesses in young gay homosexual men and injection drug users.
